92837849-a523-4613-b669-4a617f30cdab.exp: Capillaries in the CNS have a continuous basal lamina and thus lack fenestrae. In contrast to continuous capillaries in other pas of the body, they contain only a few pinocytic vesicles; this characteristic is thought to be paly responsible for the blood-brain barrier

c6db42c8-ebc4-4eb4-b841-a70a482ff103.exp: Glycine is conveed to serine by addition of methylene group from N5, N10 -methylenetetra hydrofolic acid and requires pyridoxal phosphate as coenzyme for serine hydroxymethyl tranaferase.

64b11e5e-0e62-4431-8d1c-78e374e9e040.exp: (A) Small Clear vesicle contain Ach & GABA # Inside the presynaptic terminal are many mitochondria, as well as many membrane-enclosed vesicles, which contain neurotransmitters. There are three kinds of synaptic vesicles: Clear synaptic vesicles that contain acetylcholine, glycine, GABA, or glutamate; small vesicles with a dense core that contain catecholamines; and large vesicles with a dense core that contain neuropeptides.

ed27d5d0-7610-49da-82f5-5042ac7e954d.exp: Apoptotic cells break up into fragments, called apoptotic bodies Bodies contain poions of the cytoplasm and nucleus. The plasma membrane of the apoptotic cell and bodies remains intact, but its structure is altered in such a way that phospholipids such as Phosphatidyl serine which are supposed to be present on the inner side flips outwards. This acts as a receptors for phagocytes. The dead cell and its fragments are rapidly devoured before the contents have leaked out, and therefore cell death by this pathway does not elicit an inflammatory reaction in the host.

502415af-53b4-4856-aeaf-87898665d96c.exp: Answer- A. PseudomonasPseudomonas aeruginosa is a Gram-negative, aerobic rod shaped and polar-flagella bacterium with unipolar motility.

fd2073fc-15c0-49d6-8152-d1dbf92c1ad2.exp: The clinical features are those of pericarditis with effusion, and the most common causes of hemorrhagic pericarditis are metastatic carcinoma and tuberculosis. An effusion of this size is sufficient to produce some cardiac tamponade that diminishes cardiac output; the paradoxical drop in pressure (more than 10 mm Hg) is called pulsus paradoxus and can be caused by pericarditis and by tamponade. Candida is a rare cardiac infection in immunocompromised individuals. Coxsackieviruses are known to cause myocarditis. Group A streptococci are responsible for rheumatic fever; in the acute form, rheumatic fever can lead to fibrinous pericarditis, and in the chronic form, it can lead to serous effusions from congestive heart failure. Staphylococcus aureus is most often a cause of infective endocarditis.

9c864539-9aa3-4b85-ae58-676cf0a2e625.exp: In extracapsular cataract extraction (ECCE), major poion of anterior capsule with epithelium, nucleus and coex are removed; leaving behind intact posterior capsule. So, opacificatiton of posterior capsule is the most common complication of extracapsular cataract surgery. Corneal complications and iritis/other inflammation are common post-operative complications.

f975ff25-12a4-4025-ba5f-4124c150cc9c.exp: Mondor's disease is thrombophlebitis of superficial veins of the breast and anterior chest wall. The pathogonomic feature is the thrombosed subcutaneous cord,usually attached to skin.Thevtreatment is restricted arm movements ,and in any case the condition subsides withina few months without recurrence,complications or deformity. Bailey and Love'sSho practice of surgery.Edition 23.Pg no:756

8a90662d-0bd7-46a1-863e-9562a26fa7f0.exp: Ans. is 'b' i.e., B-cells T-cell independent antigens directly stimulates B-cells without processing by antigen presenting cells.

8e40bc6e-5d86-4d43-942f-f0328abb443c.exp: H. influenza and S. pneumonia are organisms which are usually responsible for community-acquired pneumoniA. S. aureus and Pseudomonas spp. are usually found in patients with hospital-acquired pneumoniA. L. pneumophilia, along with Chlamydia spp. and Mycoplasma pneumonia, are the atypical pneumonia-causing organisms. A urinary antigen test is routinely used for the detection of Legionella spp. Serological tests can be used for the detection of Mycoplasma and Chlamydia spp. and also Legionella spp.

a70b3d62-8282-4095-a487-283e7bd35e36.exp: SARI * IMPOANCE antidepressant with good sleep, no sexual problems and no GI side effects. It was thought this would replace SSRI but dint do well because of notorious side effects * MECHANISM Serotonin antagonist and reuptake inhibitor * DRUGS Trazodone Nefazodone * USE low dose trazodone is used as a hypnotic * SIDE EFFECTS Trazodone----------- priapism Nefazodone ---------hepatotoxicity Ref. kaplon and sadock, synopsis of psychiatry, 11 th edition, pf no. 955

8275cb94-c460-4493-9b8b-1f5a4ddf724f.exp: SCHIZOPHRENIA:- Eugen Bleuler coined the term "Schizophrenia. which replaced dementia praecox in scientific literature. Bleuler proposed four symptoms which he called as fundamental (or primary) symptoms of schizophrenia. These symptoms are also known as 4 A's of Bleuler. They include: A. Autistic thinking and behavior (Autism): Excessive fantasy thinking which is irrational and withdrawn behavior. B. Ambivalence: Marked inability to take decisions. C. Affect disturbances: Disturbances of emotions such as inappropriate affect. D. Association disturbances: Disturbances of association of thoughts such as formal thought disorders. Ref:- Psychiatry by Sandeep Govil, pg num:- 80

f2bf9248-7d8c-4cbd-9aae-51702bf22a14.exp: 131I can be used for scanning for metastasis from differentiated thyroid cancers as well as for the ablation of differentiated thyroid cancer cells.

f7601172-fedb-4693-b6ad-aafe5a579c67.exp: Late expanding stage of demographic cycle is characterised by a fall in bih rate and a fuher decline in death rate. In this stage, population continues to grow because bihs exceed deaths. India is in this phase of demographic cycle.

31288d98-b7a5-4f52-9fb5-dbc1cc10d5d2.exp: Penal erasure is erasure of name of a doctor from the Medical Register on charges of seroius professional misconduct. It deprives the practitioner of all privileges of a registered practitioner. It is considered to be a professional death sentence. Professional misconduct is any conduct of the doctor which might reasonably be regarded as disgraceful or dihonourable. Conduct is judged by professional men of good repute and competence. These offences are contained in Warning Notice and his name can be erased from Medical Register if guilty. These offences include Adultery, unlawful Abortion, Advertising, Association with unqualified persons in professional matters, Addiction, Alcohol and Dichotomy or fee splitting.

5be366f0-58b6-4cff-926d-62e79592bc88.exp: Sinus tachycardia, left or right shift of QRS axis, premature atrial or ventricular beats, increasing hea size and LV mass, small pericardial effusion and mild mitral regurgitation are all normal in pregnancy. A benign ejection systolic murmur is seen in 96%of the patient. Here no treatment is needed. Ref: Philip A Kalra, 3rd edition, Page 298.

Abducent nerve: inferolateral to the internal carotid aery The structures in the infra lateral wall and on the Lasseter medial aspect of the sinus are separated from blood, by the way, endothelial lining. 40575efb-2353-4714-8c84-5964b17f7c9c.exp:
In Acoustic Neuroma

Loss of corneal reflex is seen – due to the involvement of Tringeminal nerve
Tinnitus – due to pressure on cochlear nerve
Large tumors can cause diplopia

As far as facial nerve palsy is concerned – Scott Brown 7th/ed vol-3 pg 3931
“Vestibular schwannomas, although inevitably grossly distort the VIIth nerve, very rarely present as a VIIth nerve palsy. If there is a clinical evidence of a cerebellopontine angle lesion and if the VIIth nerve is involved, alternative pathology is more likely”.
Hence although Acoustic neuroma may involve the 7 nerve but complete palsy is never seen

b04ff8ac-3cde-40f8-96aa-d73382b4de46.exp: Selivanoff's test: this is a specific test for ketohexoses. Concentrated hydrochloric acid dehydrates ketohexoses to form furfural derivatives which condense with resorcinol to give a cherry red complex.

5022afa0-df48-42b1-acb9-2808e19d12fe.exp: Fogay aerial embolectomy catheter is used to remove fresh emboli in the aerial system.It consists of a hollow tube with an inflatable balloon attached to its tip. The catheter is inseed into the blood vessel through a clot. The balloon is then inflated to extract it from the vessel.

21aa09e9-c9a5-4ac0-9451-2f487a500ca9.exp: Xeroderma pigmentosum is a genetically determined disorder, in which defective DNA repair mechanisms lead to chronic UV damage and subsequent development of different sun related skin tumors, including melanoma, in sun exposed areas.

d45e15a0-a449-47de-aeae-cd8e3a2f6197.exp: Cross Sectional Study Ecological Study Both studies are done at a point of time - Done based on primary Data i.e. collected by Investigation himself from House to House. - It provides "Prevalence", so a/K/a Prevalence Study - a/K/a "SNAPSHOT Study". - Done based on Secondary Data i.e. Collected data from records of hospital Data is not collected by Investigator himself. - Used in Nutritional Surveys - Cannot calculate strength of association / prevalence - Unit of Study is population and results are extrapolated projected not on Individuals but on total population. This drawback is known as "Ecological Fallacy".

a877f191-c281-40b0-a7d8-07071a65cc51.exp: Ans. D: Decreased depth of respirationAlthough the rhythmic discharge of medullary neurons concerned with respiration is spontaneous, it is modified by neurons in the pons and afferents in the vagus from receptors in the airways and lungs. An area known as the pneumotaxic center in the medial parabrachial and Kolliker-Fuse nuclei of the dorsolateral pons contains neurons active during inspiration and neurons active during expiration.Stretching of the lungs during inspiration initiates impulses in afferent pulmonary vagal fibers. These impulses inhibit inspiratory discharge.

8b16fec6-04a4-4359-ba9e-12c0f00ef2c1.exp: Primary fetal infection is associated with nonimmune fetal hydrops and intrauterine fetal demise,with the risk for fetal loss after infection estimated at <5%. The mechanism of fetal disease appears to be a viral-induced RBC aplasia at a time when the fetal erythroid fraction is rapidly expanding ,leading to profound anemia, high-output cardiac failure, and fetal hydrops. Nonimmune type * Three major causes -- parvovirus B 19 -- alfa-thalassemia -- cardiovascular defects, chromosomal anomalies, and fetal anemia Reference: Nelson textbook of pediatrics 19th edition, Page 1096
